title: O Leave Novels.
content:
Tune - "Mauchline belles."


I.

        O leave novels, ye Mauchline belles,
            Ye're safer at your spinning-wheel;
        Such witching books are baited hooks
            For rakish rooks, like Rob Mossgiel.

II.

        Your fine Tom Jones and Grandisons,
            They make your youthful fancies reel;
        They heat your brains, and fire your veins,
            And then you're prey for Rob Mossgiel.

III.

        Beware a tongue that's smoothly hung,
            A heart that warmly seems to feel;
        That feeling heart but acts a part,
            'Tis rakish art in Rob Mossgiel.

IV.

        The frank address, the soft caress,
            Are worse than poison'd darts of steel;
        The frank address and politesse
            Are all finesse in Rob Mossgiel.
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "O Leave Novels."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
